description:
Destructuring (`{a,b} = e`, `[h|t] = e`) flows through parser‚Üíresolve‚Üíir_lower‚Üíir_typer end to end already. The gaps are:

1. The typer correctly proves the synthesized fail block of an irrefutable destructure is dead, then emits `type/unreachable-arm`. With warnings-as-errors, this makes any real destructure unshippable.
2. The interp leg is missing the list-cons `Discriminant` primitive ‚Äî interp diverges from JIT/AOT on `[h|t] = e`.
3. No fixture exercises destructuring in a non-trivial setting.

Approach: data-model up.

DAG:

    T1 --> T2 --+
                +--> T4 --> T5
    T3 --------+

- T1 ‚Äî Outcome variant for "exhaustive match arm" + ir_typer publishes it.
- T2 ‚Äî Reducer consumer elides synthesized pattern-bind fail blocks; refutable binds keep them (BEAM-conform `match_error`).
- T3 ‚Äî Interp parity: implement list-cons Discriminant in ir_interp.
- T4 ‚Äî Parity matrix entries: irrefutable tuple, refutable list-cons, mixed nested, across all four legs.
- T5 ‚Äî fixtures/quicksort/ as the closing artefact. `{lo, hi} = partition(...)` on the hot path of a recursive sort; expected.clif should show pure projection (visible proof T2 did its job).

Constraints:
- BEAM-conform: `[h|t] = []` is a runtime `match_error`, not compile-time refusal.
- Three-path parity required (interp, JIT, AOT) + repl leg.
- Clean/simple/elegant; no shortcuts.
title: Destructuring: data-model-up, with quicksort as the closing fixture
